input.content: You are a code generator. Return only the requested JSON object. No prose.

You are running an Entropy staged code-generation benchmark.

Workload: webpage-chain
Stage: web-001-home
Order: 1

Stage goal:
Create webpage 1 as index.html with a title, main content, and an empty navigation area that can grow later.

Expected artifacts:
- site/index.html (html)

Validators that will run:
- file-exists: failure_class=missing_artifact
- html-parse: failure_class=syntax

Exact validator contract:
Generate files under site/ only.
Every HTML page must include <!doctype html>.
Required pages and literals:
- index.html: title text must include 'Entropy Page 1'
- page-2.html: title text must include 'Entropy Page 2'; backlink href must be 'index.html'
- page-3.html: title text must include 'Entropy Page 3'; backlink href must be 'page-2.html'
- page-4.html: title text must include 'Entropy Page 4'; backlink href must be 'page-3.html'
- page-5.html: title text must include 'Entropy Page 5'; backlink href must be 'page-4.html'
From page order 4, every page must link to index.html, page-2.html, page-3.html, and page-4.html.
By stage 5, index.html and page-5.html must include data-entropy-shared-table="factory-links".

Existing generated files:
FILE: 
---BEGIN FILE---

---END FILE---

Return ONLY a JSON object in this exact shape:
{
  "files": [
    { "path": "relative/path/from/run/root", "content": "complete file content" }
  ],
  "notes": "short note"
}

Rules:
- Include every new or modified file needed for this stage to pass.
- Use only relative paths.
- Do not include markdown fences outside the JSON.
- Do not explain the answer outside the JSON.
